articles.title: ‘That tells me a lot’: Alex Cora impressed by Trevor Story’s response to early-season adversity
articles.description:
"Hopefully this is the beginning of something cool for him."

Boston fans may see enhanced gameplay as Story overcomes early-season struggles.

articles.title: House hunt stories: How three Boston-area buyers navigated the market
articles.description:
The new year has brought significant changes to the housing market. The Warren Group reported an over 10 percent dip in Massachusetts single-family home sales, while the war in Iran has increased mortgage rates nationwide. As house hunters face higher property price tags, here’s how three Boston-area buyers navigated the market. A space for mom, […]

The post House hunt stories: How three Boston-area buyers navigated the market appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts single-family home sales dropped over 10% in early 2023.
Higher mortgage rates due to the Iran conflict are affecting Boston buyers.
Local house hunters are adapting to increased property prices and market challenges.

articles.title: Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school
articles.description:
Police swept Mark G. Hoyle Elementary School with a K-9 unit specially trained to detect guns and ammunition.

The post Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Swansea police recovered 5 guns from a home after a second grader brought a weapon to school.
Mark G. Hoyle Elementary School was swept by a K-9 unit for safety on April 8, 2026.
This incident raises concerns about gun safety and security in local schools for Boston residents.

articles.title: US, Israel, and Iran agree to a 2-week ceasefire, but much remains unclear and some attacks continue
articles.description:
It was not clear if the latest strikes would scuttle the deal, which U.S. Vice President JD Vance called “fragile.”

The post US, Israel, and Iran agree to a 2-week ceasefire, but much remains unclear and some attacks continue appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: US, Israel, and Iran agree to a fragile two-week ceasefire amid ongoing attacks.
Vice President JD Vance emphasizes the deal's fragility, raising concerns for regional stability.
Boston residents may face increased uncertainty regarding international relations and potential impacts on local security.
